result:
Stay consistent with wallets — mutable row is simpler, already battle-tested in your stack, and auditors only need one mental model. Event-sourced wins when you need full history, reversibility, or reconciliation against a payment processor, but you can get 90% of that by appending a gift_card_ledger table alongside the mutable balance (hybrid: balance row as cache, ledger as truth).

Real tradeoff: mutable row needs row-level locking or optimistic concurrency to prevent double-spend; event-sourced avoids that but costs you a sum-on-read (or a materialized balance, which is just the mutable row again).
